item.title: Python basics
item.description:
Build a foundation in Python.

1-2 hours per week, for 8 weeks

Learn

Quiz

What is the difference between lists and tuples?

Lists

  • Lists are mutable - they can be changed
  • Slower than tuples
  • Syntax: a_list = [1, 2.0, 'Hello world']

Tuples

  • Tuples are immutable - they can’t be changed
  • Tuples are faster than lists
  • Syntax: a_tuple = (1, 2.0, 'Hello world')

Is Python case-sensitive?

Yes

item.title: Visualization
item.description:
Learn how to visualize data with Plotly.

1-2 hours per week, for 8 weeks

Learn

Quiz

When is a heatmap useful?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

Write Plotly code to render a bar chart 

import plotly.express as px
data_canada = px.data.gapminder().query("country == 'Canada'")
fig = px.bar(data_canada, x='year', y='pop')
fig.show()

item.title: Statistics
item.description:
Introduction to statistics for data science.

1-2 hours per week, for 8 weeks

Learn

The general form of the normal probability density function is:

$$ f(x) = \frac{1}{\sigma \sqrt{2\pi} } e^{-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^2} $$

The parameter $\mu$ is the mean or expectation of the distribution. $\sigma$ is its standard deviation. The variance of the distribution is $\sigma^{2}$.

Quiz

What is the parameter $\mu$?

The parameter $\mu$ is the mean or expectation of the distribution.

item.title: Paper Session at Sase 2022
Known for being different? Reputation, Status, and the Optimal Distinctiveness of New Ventures
item.description: We investigate how audience evaluation of new ventures’ distinctiveness changes over time, i.e., when audiences become more familiar and less uncertain about them.Using longitudinal data on 4,279 products offered by 853 new ventures over 211 weeks on Amazon Launchpad, we observe familiarity through status, i.e., new ventures increasing establishment in the respective product category, and uncertainty through their reputation, i.e., the increasingly available evidence on past performance. Building on the notion of optimal distinctiveness as the trade-off between legitimacy and differentiation, we propose that both status and reputation steepen the U-shaped effect of new ventures’ distinctiveness. However, while highly distinct ventures benefit the most, new ventures face an ever increasing “stuck in the middle” problem that becomes particularly problematic when audiences become more familiar and less uncertain about averagely distinct ventures. Our results show that audience evaluation of a new venture’s distinctiveness is not only contingent on preferences or competitive and categorical contexts, but also by evolving degrees of complementary organizational traits. From a managerial perspective, our results provide guidance on how to achieve optimal distinctiveness while evolving into more mature market actors. Managers need to consider that although they may have found a favorable degree of distinctiveness when entering the overall market this may change as their status or reputation grows.

item.title: Cultural Entrepreneurship
item.description: In this project, my co-authors and I focus on cultural entrepreneurship as a strategic resource. In particular, we intend to find out how new ventures and entrepreneurs alike can use texts or visuals to influence evaluating audiences, such as consumers or investors. A prime illustration for this idea is our 2020 paper in JBV, where we look at Indie Game Developers and how they try to narrate their game idea to potential buyers. Most recently, our work focusses on non-written narratives, such as visual or spoken ones. We will hopefully be able to share some working papers in the very near future.

item.title: Optimal Distinctiveness
item.description: In this project, my co-authors and I focus on how new ventures achieve optimal distinctiveness, i.e., how they become as unique as possible while still being perceived as a legitimate market actor. Much of our work in this project addresses audience evaluation and special category contexts. At the moment, we particularly focus on how optimal distinctiveness evolves over time and how it works in the context of international markets. We will upload these working papers as soon as they are ready. With some papers under review, we hope to be able to share some good news soon.

item.title: Symposium on Entrepreneurial Strategy at VHB2022
Paying for Legitimacy? The Signalling Effect of Monetary Rewards in Innovation Contests
New conference talks
item.description:
New year, new conference season!

My recent study with Stephie has been accepted at the SASE 2022 and the AOM 2022 Annual Conference! If you want to meet up in beautiful Amsterdam and/or Seattle make sure to drop me a mail!

item.title: Welcome to my page
item.description:
New page launched!

I am very happy that I have finally found the time to compile this new site that focuses on my work as a researcher. On it, you can find announcements for talks, teaching materials, papers, data, code and everything else that has to do with my work. Also, I will share some (hopefully) nifty tricks that I learned in my ten years as an empirical researcher that utilizes large-scale secondary data.

Enjoy reading and browsing and feel free to drop me a mail in case you have any questions!

item.title: More than words! How narrative anchoring and enrichment help to balance differentiation and conformity of entrepreneurial products
Slides
item.description:
Create slides in Markdown with Wowchemy

Wowchemy | Documentation

Features

  • Efficiently write slides in Markdown
  • 3-in-1: Create, Present, and Publish your slides
  • Supports speaker notes
  • Mobile friendly slides

Controls

  • Next: Right Arrow or Space
  • Previous: Left Arrow
  • Start: Home
  • Finish: End
  • Overview: Esc
  • Speaker notes: S
  • Fullscreen: F
  • Zoom: Alt + Click
  • PDF Export: E

Code Highlighting

Inline code: variable

Code block:

porridge = "blueberry"
if porridge == "blueberry":
    print("Eating...")

Math

In-line math: $x + y = z$

Block math:

$$ f\left( x \right) = ;\frac{{2\left( {x + 4} \right)\left( {x - 4} \right)}}{{\left( {x + 4} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right)}} $$

Fragments

Make content appear incrementally

{{% fragment %}} One {{% /fragment %}}
{{% fragment %}} **Two** {{% /fragment %}}
{{% fragment %}} Three {{% /fragment %}}

Press Space to play!

One **Two** Three

A fragment can accept two optional parameters:

  • class: use a custom style (requires definition in custom CSS)
  • weight: sets the order in which a fragment appears

Speaker Notes

Add speaker notes to your presentation

{{% speaker_note %}}
- Only the speaker can read these notes
- Press `S` key to view
{{% /speaker_note %}}

Press the S key to view the speaker notes!

  • Only the speaker can read these notes
  • Press S key to view

Themes

  • black: Black background, white text, blue links (default)
  • white: White background, black text, blue links
  • league: Gray background, white text, blue links
  • beige: Beige background, dark text, brown links
  • sky: Blue background, thin dark text, blue links
  • night: Black background, thick white text, orange links
  • serif: Cappuccino background, gray text, brown links
  • simple: White background, black text, blue links
  • solarized: Cream-colored background, dark green text, blue links

Custom Slide

Customize the slide style and background

{{< slide background-image="/media/boards.jpg" >}}
{{< slide background-color="#0000FF" >}}
{{< slide class="my-style" >}}

Custom CSS Example

Let’s make headers navy colored.

Create assets/css/reveal_custom.css with:

.reveal section h1,
.reveal section h2,
.reveal section h3 {
  color: navy;
}

Questions?

Ask

Documentation

item.title: Privacy Policy/ Datenschutz
item.description:
Datenschutz

Ich speichere keine Informationen über die Besucher:innen dieser Webseite.

Die Webseite wird bei GitHub Pages gehostet. Zu umfassenden Informationen zu den von GitHub erhobenen und gespeicherten Daten siehe das GitHub Privacy Statement. In Bezug auf GitHub Pages heißt es dort: Please note that GitHub may collect User Personal Information from visitors to your GitHub Pages website, including logs of visitor IP addresses, to comply with legal obligations, and to maintain the security and integrity of the Website and the Service.

Privacy Information

I do not store any information about the visitors of this website.

This website is hosted by GitHub Pages. For more detailed information on the data collected and stored by GitHub please refer to the GitHub Privacy Statement. In terms of GitHub pages it says: Please note that GitHub may collect User Personal Information from visitors to your GitHub Pages website, including logs of visitor IP addresses, to comply with legal obligations, and to maintain the security and integrity of the Website and the Service.

item.title: An Upbeat Crowd. Fast In-store Music Alleviates Negative Effects of High Social Density on Customers’ Spending
Paying for Legitimacy? The Signalling Effect of Monetary Rewards in Innovation Contests
